column1: # PB-LINK-1 golden corpus — internal link resolution (§A2).
# NOT YET FROZEN — chunk-2 free-correction window. Rows 1-29 transcribed from the §A2 golden table
# BEFORE any flexmark code existed (anti-circularity); rows 30-31 added in the free window from the
# amended §A2 (B2 asset-query preservation, B3 undecodable-fragment -> broken_malformed), authored
# from the SPEC then validated by the contract-smoke spike. URLs use §A4 canonical-URL construction over
# the fixture tree, computed by the stub PageIndexView in the test (no _folder.yaml slug
# overrides in fixtures; deploy-guide.md carries slug: deploy-guide; root index.md carries
# slug: welcome). Run against the pure-domain LinkResolver with a stub index.
# Columns (tab-separated):
#   row        the §A2 row number (for traceability).
#   source     content-relative file path of the page the link is written in.
#   link       the link href exactly as written in Markdown.
#   outcome    one of: page, asset, external, anchor, blocked_scheme, broken_missing,
#              broken_case_mismatch, broken_malformed, outside_content_root, ambiguous.
#   url_or_hint  for page/asset: the emitted URL. For external/anchor: the verbatim
#              passed-through value. For broken_case_mismatch: the candidate path hint.
#              For other broken classes: empty (a single literal `-`).
# row
# B2 (chunk-2 free-correction window): asset ?query is PRESERVED for assets (raw, never decoded),
# appended before any fragment. §A2 step 1 + output-form table; §A4 asset URL = /assets/{root-rel}.
# B3 (chunk-2 free-correction window, owner-ratified): an undecodable fragment fails strict decode
# -> broken_malformed (symmetric with the path; never emitted raw). The space + lone `%` in
# `100% done` cannot strict-decode (`% d` is not a valid %XX escape).
# B4 (chunk-2 free-correction window): a SAME-PAGE `#fragment` goes through the SAME strict fragment
# decode/re-encode as page/asset fragments (§A2 step 1, owner-ratified). Row 32: a valid unicode anchor
# `#café` strict-decodes then re-encodes per RFC 3986 on the wire -> `#caf%C3%A9` (NEVER emitted raw).
# Row 33: an undecodable same-page fragment (`caf%E9` — lone `%E9` is not valid strict UTF-8) fails the
# same strict decode the path/asset fragments use -> broken_malformed (never emitted raw). This is the
# one path that previously skipped the strict decode and returned the raw fragment as a resolved anchor.
# B5 (chunk-2 free-correction window): an EXTENSIONLESS wrong-case link to an existing PAGE. `Setup`
# resolves to `guides/Setup`; the `.md` probe (`guides/Setup.md`) and asset probe (`guides/Setup`) both
# miss case-sensitively, so the rescue scan (§A2 step 6) runs against the INFERRED `.md` page candidate
# (`guides/Setup.md`), which matches `guides/setup.md` case-insensitively -> broken_case_mismatch (NOT
# broken_missing). Mirrors row 11's `.md` case-mismatch, for the extensionless inferred-page form.
